text:
Ideal Requirement: Students should maintain 100% attendance in lectures, labs, and tutorials.
Minimum Threshold (Undergraduate): 80%.
Minimum Threshold (Graduate/PhD): 75%.
Condonation: Up to 20% of total lectures can be condoned for genuine reasons like illness.
Penalty: Failure to meet these requirements results in an F/A (Fail due to Attendance) grade and ineligibility for final exams.

2. Academic Grading System
Performance is typically split 50% for semester work (midterms, quizzes, etc.) and 50% for final examinations.

W: Withdrawn (No GPA impact).
I: Incomplete (Converts to F if not completed by next semester).

3. Honors & Recognition
Rector’s List: SGPA of 4.00 (Undergraduates only).
Dean’s List: SGPA between 3.50 and 3.99.

Summa Cum Laude: CGPA 3.90 – 4.00.
Magna Cum Laude: CGPA 3.75 – 3.89.
Cum Laude: CGPA 3.60 – 3.74.

4. Academic Warning & Probation

section: Warnings are issued if CGPA falls below
text:
BS/BBA: 2.00.
MS/MBA: 2.50.
PhD: 3.00.
Rule of Three: If the Warning Count reaches three, the student’s admission is closed.

5. Course Rules (FYP & Summer)
Final Year Project (FYP): To register for FYP-I, a student must have passed all courses from the first six semesters (deficiency of 1-2 courses may be allowed by HOD/Director).
Summer Semester: An optional 8–9 week session. Maximum of 2 courses allowed. Fees are non-refundable.

6. Degree Completion Limits

7. Disciplinary Action
Academic Dishonesty: Minimum punishment is zero marks in the relevant task; maximum is an 'F' grade in the course or expulsion.
Medium of Instruction: English is the standard for all exams, except for Religious/Islamic studies which can be answered in Urdu.
Admission to the university is strictly based on merit. There are no quotas, reserved seats, or admissions through donation. There is also no age limit for applicants.
The university’s own admission test, or

Admission Fee: Rs. 30,000 (payable once at the time of admission).
Note: Graduates of FAST-NUCES admitted to a higher degree program do not pay this again.
Security Deposit: Rs. 20,000 (refundable upon degree completion or closing of admission).
Applicant Admission Fee: Rs. 3,000.

2. Recurring Semester Fees
Tuition Fee (All Programs/Campuses): Rs. 11,000 per credit hour.
Student Activities Fund: Rs. 2,500 per semester.
Late Payment Fine: Charged at a rate of Re 1 per day for every thousand rupees of the total outstanding balance.

3. Miscellaneous Service Fees

4. Fee Refund Policy
Refunds for Tuition and Student Activity funds follow HEC guidelines. The Admission Fee is never refundable.
Calculation Note: Timelines include weekends and holidays.
Eligibility Failure: If a student's admission is cancelled because they failed to meet eligibility requirements (after results), the refund timeline is calculated from the date of the result announcement.

section: The university offers several forms of direct financial support
text:
Merit Scholarship for Bachelors: Awarded to the Top Three position holders of each Examination Board in their year of admission.
Semester-Wise Merit Awards: Cash prizes and certificates are given to undergraduate students who rank in the top three of their department each semester.
Eligibility: Students must have a regular course load and an SGPA of 3.00 or higher.
Study Loans: Interest-free loans are arranged for bright students who cannot afford the fees.
Coverage: Limited to tuition fees only.

Bachelors: Maximum of 8 semesters.
Masters: Maximum of 4 semesters.
PhD: Maximum of 6 semesters.
Restrictions: Cannot be used for repeat or withdrawn courses; it only covers a course once.
Academic Standing: Sponsorship is suspended if a student's CGPA falls into the warning zone but is restored once the CGPA improves.

2.

3. Need-Based & Community Assistance
OSAF & FANA: Financial assistance provided by the Old Students Association of FAST and the FAST Alumni of North America.
Ihsan Trust: Offers financial assistance for students unable to pay full fees.
General Refaqat Memorial: Specifically for students facing financial hardship.
Community-Based: Various foundations including Memon, Bohra, and Agha Khan Foundation offer specialized scholarships.

The university provides transport services on a self-finance or subsidized basis across its campuses.
Note for Peshawar Hostilities: A shuttle to/from the Daewoo/Bus terminal is available at designated times on Fridays and Mondays.

2. Hostel Facilities
On-campus housing is available at specific campuses with varying capacities and amenities.

Capacity: Accommodates over 800 students and faculty in separate hostels for boys and girls.
Amenities: Well-equipped mess, study room, food storage (freezer/oven), internet, iron facility, medical center, and a gymnasium/fitness center.
Recreation: Table tennis and billiard boards.

On-Campus (Boys Only): Capacity for 150 students.
Room Features: Each student is provided with a bed, bedside table, study table, and a lockable cupboard.
General Facilities: Laundry, ironing, campus internet, and weekly pick/drop to city bus terminals on weekends.
External Housing: Campus-managed hostels are also available within walking distance for additional capacity.
